chapter: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: The pair of linear equations $x = 5$ and $y = -3$ graphically represents lines which are

steps: Identify the nature of the lines. The equation $x = 5$ represents a vertical line parallel to the $y$-axis passing through $(5, 0)$.
The equation $y = -3$ represents a horizontal line parallel to the $x$-axis passing through $(0, -3)$.
Observe that a vertical line and a horizontal line must intersect at exactly one point.
The point of intersection is found by taking the $x$-coordinate from the first equation and the $y$-coordinate from the second equation, which is $(5, -3)$.
solution.derivation: $x = 5 \text{ is a vertical line} \\ y = -3 \text{ is a horizontal line} \\ \text{A vertical line and a horizontal line intersect at point } (5, -3) \\ \text{Therefore, they are intersecting lines at } (5, -3)$
